Enter each segment of the claim path as: Enter each segment of the claim path as a separate item. Simple claims like "email" require only one segment. For nested claims like "user.name", add multiple segments in order: first "user", then "name".
Each segment must start with a letter or: Each segment must start with a letter or underscore and contain only letters, numbers, dots or underscores.

Separator for org:role format (default:: Separator for org:role format (default: ":"). Roles containing the separator are split into organization-scoped roles. Roles without separator are global and apply to all organizations. Example: "org1:flightctl-admin" becomes org-scoped role "flightctl-admin" for organization "org1", while "flightctl-admin" becomes a global role.

Set the maximum CPU usage for your conta: Set the maximum CPU usage for your container. Use fractional values ("0.5" for half a CPU core) or whole numbers ("1", "2" for full cores). Consider your device's total CPU capacity when setting limits.
Provide a valid CPU value (e.g., \"0.4\": Provide a valid CPU value (e.g., "0.4" or "2").
Set the maximum memory usage for your co: Set the maximum memory usage for your container using Podman format. You can specify a number with an optional unit: "b" (bytes), "k" (kibibytes), "m" (mebibytes), "g" (gibibytes). Examples: "512", "512m", "1g", "2048k". Ensure the limit fits within your device's available memory and accounts for other applications and system processes.

(0777) Read, write, and execute permissi: (0777) Read, write, and execute permissions for all users.
(0755) Read and execute permission for a: (0755) Read and execute permission for all users. The file's owner also has write permission.
(0750) Read and execute permission for t: (0750) Read and execute permission for the owner and group. The file's owner also has write permission.
(0700) Read, write, and execute permissi: (0700) Read, write, and execute permissions for the file's owner only.
(0666) Read and write permissions for al: (0666) Read and write permissions for all users. No execute permissions for others.
(0664) Read and write permissions for th: (0664) Read and write permissions for the owner and group. Read-only permission for all others.
(0660) Read and write permissions for th: (0660) Read and write permissions for the owner and group.
(0644) Read and write permissions for th: (0644) Read and write permissions for the owner. Read-only permission for all others.
(0640) Read and write permissions for th: (0640) Read and write permissions for the owner, and read-only permission for the group.
(0600) Read and write permissions for th: (0600) Read and write permissions for the owner.

Rollout policies allow you to control th: Rollout policies allow you to control the order of updates for the fleet devices.
Disruption budget allows you to limit th: Disruption budget allows you to limit the number of similar devices that may be updating simultaneously.
All the devices that are part of this fl: All the devices that are part of this fleet will receive updates as soon as they are available.
The disruption budget will be applied to: The disruption budget will be applied to all group combinations independently.
Use only the 'key' part of the labels to: Use only the 'key' part of the labels to group same values.
Leaving this empty will apply the disrup: Leaving this empty will apply the disruption budget to all fleet's devices.
At least this number of devices will be: At least this number of devices will be available at any given moment, for each group defined above.
No more than this number of devices will: No more than this number of devices will be unavailable at any given moment, for each group defined above.
The minimum percentage of devices that m: The minimum percentage of devices that must be updated successfully in order to continue updating the next batch of devices.
% of the batch devices pass the success: % of the batch devices pass the success threshold, move to next batch or the rest of fleet's devices.
Batches will be applied from first to la: Batches will be applied from first to last.
Devices that are not part of any batch w: Devices that are not part of any batch will be updated last.
The time-frame within which a device mus: The time-frame within which a device must be updated. If it exceeds it, device will be counted as a 'failed to update' in the batch success threshold.
Timeout devices that fail to update afte: Timeout devices that fail to update after
Time frame during which devices can star: Time frame during which devices can start downloading their new configurations.
Time frame during which devices can start downloading and installing their new configurations.
Time frame during which devices can start installing their new configurations.
Devices can start the update within this: Devices can start the update within this time window after the scheduled trigger.
Use different update schedules for downl: Use different update schedules for downloading and installing updates
<0>{fleetId}</0> will be deleted permane: <0>{fleetId}</0> will be deleted permanently. If the device selector of a remaining fleet matches a device currently linked to this fleet, the device will be moved to the new fleet. If there's no matching fleet for a device, it will be unlinked from any fleet.
Are you sure you want to delete?: Are you sure you want to delete?
This fleet is managed by a resource sync: This fleet is managed by a resource sync and cannot be directly deleted. Either remove this fleet's definition from the resource sync configuration, or delete the resource sync first.
You are about to resume all<1>{suspended: You are about to resume <1>{suspendedDevicesCount}</1> suspended device in <4>{fleetId}</4>
You are about to resume all<1>{suspendedDevicesCount}</1> suspended devices in <4>{fleetId}</4>
Fleets make it easier to manage multiple: Fleets make it easier to manage multiple devices with shared configurations.
Fleets allow you to edit and update your: Fleets allow you to edit and update your devices at once.
To get started, create a new fleet or im: To get started, create a new fleet or import an existing configuration.
A resource sync is an automated Gitops m: A resource sync is an automated Gitops method that helps manage your imported fleets by monitoring source repository changes and updating your fleet configuration accordingly.
Fleets will appear in the fleets table l: Fleets will appear in the fleets table list and their status will be reflecting the resource sync process status. After a few minutes, they should be synced and enabled.
